label: Map / Reduce
description: Fan out over an input collection — run the downstream subgraph once per item under a concurrency limit — then reduce the per-item results into a single value (collect, concat, sum, avg, min, max, count).

settings.name: items
settings.label: Collection
settings.description: Path/template that resolves to the array to map over. When unset, maps over the upstream input directly (which must be an array).

settings.name: itemVariable
settings.label: Item variable
settings.description: Name of the per-item variable bound on each subgraph run (defaults to 'item').

settings.name: concurrency
settings.label: Concurrency
settings.description: Maximum number of per-item subgraph executions running at once. 1 = sequential. Raise to parallelize fan-out under a bound.

settings.name: reduce
settings.label: Reduce strategy
settings.description: How to fold per-item results. collect=array of results; concat=flatten arrays / join strings; sum/avg/min/max=numeric reductions; count=number of items.

ai.shortDescription: Fan out a step over every item in a list, then aggregate the results.
ai.whenToUse: When the same downstream chain must run for each element of a collection and you want a single aggregated output — e.g. score every document then sum the scores, or enrich every record then collect them.
ai.examplePrompt: for each record in steps.query.output, run the enrichment step with up to 5 in parallel, then collect the results
ai.promptHints: Set items to the dotted path of the array. Inside the subgraph reference the current element via input (primitives) or input.<itemVariable> (objects). Output: {{steps.X.output}} is the reduced value, {{steps.X.mapped}} is the raw per-item array.

outputAssertions.name: map_reduce_shape
outputAssertions.errorMessage: map_reduce returned a malformed result — expected { mapped: unknown[], itemCount: number, output }. An empty collection (itemCount: 0) is a valid outcome.
